title: Brewey
tagline: Coffee brewing tracker for home baristas
description: A native mobile app that turns coffee brewing into a craft you can measure. Log every pour-over with multi-phase recipes, manage your bean cellar bag by bag, and watch personalized insights reveal your favorite methods, peak brew days, and how your ratings trend over time.
overview: Dial in every cup — log your brews, track your beans, and learn what makes your best coffee.
technologies: React Native, Expo, TypeScript, Supabase, PostgreSQL, React Query

demo.heading: A 60-second walkthrough
demo.body: Watch a brew come together end to end — pick a bean from your cellar, build a phased pour-over recipe, rate the result, then jump to Insights to see your methods and rating trend take shape.
points: Bean cellar with per-bag remaining weight
Insights: favorite method, peak day, rating trend

problem: Serious home brewing lives in scattered notes — grind setting in one app, water temp in your head, bean roast dates on the bag, and no way to tell which variables actually made yesterday's cup great.
solution: One native app that captures the full recipe for every brew, tracks each bean down to the gram, and turns that history into insights — so you can repeat your best cups instead of guessing.

process.title: Define the brew model
process.description: Mapped how a brew, its phases, the bean and the bag relate before writing any UI.

process.title: Schema-first in Postgres
process.description: Built the data layer as Supabase migrations with row-level security from day one.

process.title: Retire the backend
process.description: Replaced a standalone FastAPI server with direct Supabase access, moving stats into SQL views and RPCs.

process.title: Craft the editorial UI
process.description: Designed a serif-and-sage native interface with React Query handling all server state.

screens.caption: Coffee detail — bags and brew history
Insights — methods, peak day, rating trend
Bean cellar — your coffees at a glance

architecture.summary: A native React Native (Expo) client talking directly to Supabase for auth and Postgres data, with React Query managing caching and server state. Bag aggregates and user stats are computed in-database via SQL views and RPCs, all guarded by row-level security.

keyFeatures.title: Phased Brew Logging
keyFeatures.description: Log each brew as a full recipe — dose, yield, grinder setting, water temp and a timeline of pour phases (bloom, pours, drawdown) with per-phase water and timing.

keyFeatures.title: Bean Cellar & Bags
keyFeatures.description: Track every coffee down to the bag, with roast date, purchase price and a live remaining-weight gauge so you always know what's fresh and how much is left.

keyFeatures.title: Specialty Coffee Detail
keyFeatures.description: Capture origin, region, elevation, farm, producer, process, variety, cupping score and flavour notes — including multi-component records for blends.

keyFeatures.title: Brew Ratings & History
keyFeatures.description: Rate each cup and browse a full brew history per coffee, so you can see exactly which recipe produced your best results.

keyFeatures.title: Personalized Insights
keyFeatures.description: Week, month and year views surface your favorite method, best bean, peak brewing day, cups-per-day chart, rating trend and top flavours.

keyFeatures.title: Brew Streaks & Stats
keyFeatures.description: A day-streak counter and headline stats (average rating, total brews, beans owned) computed in-database keep your ritual visible at a glance.

keyFeatures.title: Grinders & Methods
keyFeatures.description: Reusable grinders and brew methods — global defaults plus your own — remember your last-used setup so logging is fast.

keyFeatures.title: Secure Per-User Data
keyFeatures.description: Email/password auth and Postgres row-level security ensure every user only ever sees and edits their own coffees, bags and brews.

challenges.title: Modeling a Brew Recipe
challenges.description: A single brew isn't one record — it's a recipe with an ordered set of pour phases, linked to a bean and a specific bag. Designing relational tables that captured this without making logging tedious took several iterations.

challenges.title: Retiring the FastAPI Backend
challenges.description: The app originally talked to a standalone FastAPI server. Migrating to direct Supabase access meant reimplementing bag aggregates and user stats as in-database SQL views and RPCs while keeping the client contract identical.

challenges.title: Row-Level Security
challenges.description: Every table, view and function had to be scoped to auth.uid() so users can never read or write another person's data — including getting security_invoker views and SECURITY INVOKER functions right so RLS isn't accidentally bypassed.

challenges.title: Computing Streaks in SQL
challenges.description: Calculating a consecutive-day brewing streak entirely in Postgres — handling gaps, today-vs-yesterday edges and time zones — was a fun window-function challenge.

challenges.title: Server State with React Query
challenges.description: Keeping the bean cellar, brew list and stats in sync after every mutation required careful query-key design and cache invalidation so the UI always reflected the latest data.

challenges.title: An Editorial Native UI
challenges.description: Building a distinctive serif-and-sage aesthetic in React Native — custom fonts, an immersive gradient hero, hairline dividers and hand-drawn SVG charts — while keeping it fast and native-feeling.

techStacks.name: React Native
techStacks.description: The cross-platform framework powering the native iOS and Android client with a single TypeScript codebase.

techStacks.name: Expo
techStacks.description: Tooling and runtime for building, running and shipping the app, including font loading and native modules.

techStacks.name: TypeScript
techStacks.description: Provides end-to-end type safety from the Supabase table shapes through to every screen and component.

techStacks.name: Supabase
techStacks.description: Backend platform providing authentication and a hosted Postgres database accessed directly from the app.

techStacks.name: PostgreSQL
techStacks.description: Stores all data and computes bag aggregates, user stats and brew streaks via SQL views and RPC functions, secured by row-level security.

techStacks.name: React Query
techStacks.description: Manages all server state — caching, background refetching and cache invalidation after mutations — for a snappy, always-fresh UI.
